item.title: European Stock Rally Halts as Earnings Kick Into Gear
Italy Gives €211 Million to Data Center Tech Firm 2D Photonics
item.description: Italy awarded a €211 million ($249 million) grant to a company seeking to speed up processing at artificial intelligence data centers as part of a bid to support local startups.

item.title: Victory Giant Is Said Set to Price Hong Kong Listing at Top of Range
item.description: Victory Giant Technology Huizhou Co. is likely to price its Hong Kong listing at the highest possible figure, according to people familiar with the matter, in a sign of strong demand for the city’s biggest share sale in seven months.

item.title: ASML Raises 2026 Sales Forecast as AI Demand Fuels Growth
item.description: ASML Holding NV raised its full-year sales forecast as the surge in global artificial intelligence spending fuels semiconductor production and boosts demand for the company’s advanced chipmaking machines.

item.title: Japan May Extend $10 Billion to Southeast Asia to Help Buy Oil
item.description: Japan plans to provide as much as $10 billion in financial support to nations in Southeast Asia to help them cope with soaring crude oil prices due to the war in the Middle East, according to local media reports.

item.title: South Korea’s Naver Prices First Euro Bond Amid Push Into AI
item.description: South Korean platform giant Naver Corp. has sold a combined $1.1 billion of green international bonds, including its inaugural euro-denominated bond, as the company continues its bet on artificial-intelligence investments.

item.title: Singapore Home Sales Rebound as Project Launches Attract Buyers
item.description: Singapore new home sales rose in March as fresh project launches drew buyers back to the market after a slow start to the year.

item.title: India’s RBI Holds Talks With Banks on Ways to Boost Deposits
item.description: India’s central bank is seeking input from commercial lenders on how they can bring in larger and more stable deposits, as a shift in household savings into equities and other investment products threatens to snowball into a bigger problem for the country’s banks.

item.title: Yellen Still Sees Chance of a Fed Cut This Year Amid Oil Shock
item.description: Former Treasury Secretary and ex-Federal Reserve Chair Janet Yellen said she still sees prospects for a US interest-rate cut later this year, though the unfolding oil shock caused by the war in Iran clouds the outlook.

item.title: Asset Management One CEO Says Non-Japan Funds to Double by 2028
item.description: Mizuho Financial Group Inc.’s money management arm expects strong demand from overseas investors looking to Japan to drive asset growth in the coming years.

item.title: Lululemon Sparks China Social Media Debate On ‘Forever Chemicals’
item.description: A Texas investigation into Lululemon Athletica Inc.’s clothing for the presence of so-called “forever chemicals” has sparked a wave of debate across Chinese social media, underscoring the risks the brand faces in one of its most important growth markets.

item.title: For Markets, the War Might as Well Be Over
item.description: Stocks are nearly back to their all-time highs from the months before the conflict.

item.title: Nvidia’s New AI Models Spark Rally in Quantum Computing Stocks
item.description: Asian software and information-technology stocks surged after Nvidia Corp. unveiled a suite of new open-source AI models aimed at accelerating progress within quantum computing.

item.title: Japan’s Asahi Kasei Says Seeking Alternative Sources of Naphtha
item.description: Japanese chemicals maker Asahi Kasei Corp. says it’s working to diversify sources of naphtha as the war in the Middle East curbs supplies.

item.title: Pakistan Secures $3 Billion in Saudi Loans to Lift Reserves
item.description: Pakistan said it secured $3 billion in financial support from Saudi Arabia, helping to bolster its foreign exchange reserves and offset a loan repayment the country will make to the United Arab Emirates.

item.title: Iraq-Bound Tanker Attempts Hormuz Crossing Into Persian Gulf
item.description: An Iraq-bound supertanker that aborted a weekend attempt to cross the Strait of Hormuz into the Persian Gulf is now sailing through the waterway, a transit that would make it the first crude carrier to head west through the conduit since a US blockade took effect on Monday.

item.title: China’s Petrochemical Producers Idle Capacity as Margins Crumble
item.description: China’s petrochemical producers, which supply textile and plastics factories, have cut operations to their lowest seasonal level in three years as rising feedstock costs and soft export demand squeezes margins.

item.title: Singapore Bonds Surge Past Treasuries on Liquidity, Haven Demand
item.description: Singapore government bonds are extending their outperformance over US Treasuries to levels not seen since 2007, as a drop in local interbank borrowing costs points to a surge in haven demand.

item.title: Anthropic’s Mythos Is a Wake-up Call For Everyone, Not Just Banks
item.description: Mythos, a new artificial intelligence model that Anthropic PBC has teased as too dangerous to release, looked at first like a problem for banks. Days after the company announced the new technology, US Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent summoned Wall Street leaders to make sure they were taking precautions to defend their systems, creating invaluable publicity for Anthropic and raising questions about who gets an exclusive peek at its threatening progeny.

item.title: China’s Xi Meets Russia’s Foreign Minister Lavrov in Beijing
item.description: Chinese President Xi Jinping met with visiting Russian Foreign Minister Sergei Lavrov in Beijing, according to the official Xinhua News Agency, as the two countries seek closer strategic coordination with the Middle East conflict dragging on.

item.title: Below-Average Monsoon May Delay Recovery in India Consumer Demand
item.description: A longer summer could benefit makers of ice-creams, dairy drinks, and soft beverages.

item.title: SoftBank Lenders Ask More Banks to Join $40 Billion OpenAI Loan
item.description: More banks are being invited to join SoftBank Group Corp.’s $40 billion loan backing its investment in US tech giant OpenAI, in one of the biggest tests yet of creditor sentiment toward the Japanese conglomerate’s debt-fueled push further into artificial intelligence.

item.title: Copper Erases Wartime Loss as Traders Eye Peace Talks and Demand
item.description: Copper advanced to erase losses sustained since the war in the Middle East began more than six weeks ago, buoyed by renewed plans for peace talks and demand from electrification projects.

item.title: China Plans Largest Yuan Bond Issuance Since 2023 in Hong Kong
item.description: China is planning to issue the largest yuan sovereign bonds in Hong Kong since 2023 this month, increasing supply to global investors just as yuan assets stand out as a haven amid the Iran war.

item.title: Memory Crunch and Iran War Lead Phone Market Decline, IDC Says
item.description: The global smartphone market suffered its first decline since 2023 in the first quarter of this year, according to market tracker IDC, with the memory chip supply crunch and war in Iran likely to elevate costs and constrain growth further.

item.title: BOK Governor Nominee Warns of Inflation, Flags Excess Won Moves
item.description: South Korea’s central bank governor nominee Shin Hyun Song warned that soaring oil prices and a weaker won are likely to push inflation higher, while weighing on economic growth, highlighting the policy challenges he is likely to face at the start of his tenure.

item.title: China’s Aluminum Exports to Surge as Mideast Disruptions Persist
item.description: Chinese aluminum exports are expected to surge in coming months as buyers look for other sources of the metal to offset disruptions in the Persian Gulf.

item.title: Hedge Fund MS Capital Says It Won $1 Billion Mandate for China
item.description: Quantitative hedge fund Meridian & Saturn Capital said it won a $1 billion mandate to trade Chinese stocks, another sign investors are boosting allocations to the world’s second-largest economy.

item.title: Philippines Grants Relief for Borrowers, Banks Amid Iran War Pain
item.description: The Philippine central bank said it’s providing relief measures to banks and their clients, including longer repayment periods, as the nation grapples with the fallout from the Middle East conflict.

item.title: Traders Boost Taiwan Stock Leverage to Highest in 25 Years
item.description: Leveraged bets on Taiwanese stocks climbed to their highest level in more than two decades as lingering uncertainty over the Iran war failed to deter investors from pushing the benchmark to new highs.
